description_long: This is a large, striking flower with a tall, central spadix surrounded by a flared, funnel-shaped spathe. The spathe is ribbed and transitions from a deep maroon or burgundy at the upper edges to a pale greenish-yellow at the base. The spadix is elongated, pale yellow-green, and rises prominently from the center of the spathe. The base of the flower is thick and bulbous, sitting atop a broad, irregularly shaped green structure that resembles a leafy pad or bract. The surface of the spathe appears slightly textured, with visible ridges and undulations. This flower is reminiscent of the Amorphophallus titanum, commonly known as the corpse flower, and would be a dramatic feature in an urban park or botanical garden.
description: A large, dramatic flower with a tall central spadix and a ribbed, maroon-to-green spathe, sitting atop a broad leafy base. The structure is bold and visually striking, ideal for a botanical display.
description_view_0: Front view shows the open, flared spathe with deep maroon edges and the tall, pale spadix rising from the center.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the ribbed texture of the spathe and the curvature of the leafy base.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the spathe, with more greenish tones and the spadix extending upward.
description_view_3: Another side view, showing the pale inner surface of the spathe and the irregular, leafy base.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The spathe forms a single, large, funnel-shaped structure that wraps partially around the central spadix, displaying a radial and slightly whorled arrangement with pronounced ribbing and overlapping edges.
